what: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: Audience: next agent (developer) extending htna. Status: Draft, derived 2026-04-26 design review cograph 2.1.1. Scope: htna layer top cograph Nestimate — belongs , stays upstream, templates building .

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: cograph generic plotting engine. can render single networks multi-group networks (plot_htna), opinion “group” means. htna package knows actor partitions real need consistent visual identity across plots. Therefore: - cograph exposes raw params (eventually) layout helpers. htna knowledge. - htna owns: actor-partition styling, default layout choices actor partitions, multi-plot layout consistency (bootstrap plot base plot look like network), htna class chain. - htna wrappers compose: read partition x$node_groups → merge htna styling defaults user ... → call cograph::* explicit args. Anti-pattern avoid: writing htna-specific styling (e.g. htna_styling = TRUE) cograph. couples cograph’s release cadence htna’s visual decisions, sets precedent produced tna_styling/psych_styling historical drift cograph.

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: build_htna() writes partition two places returned netobject: x$nodes$groups — column nodes data frame; cograph’s plot_htna auto-detects name. Kept backward-compat users call cograph::plot_htna(net) directly. x$node_groups — canonical schema: data frame columns node group. htna’s wrappers MUST read , $nodes$groups, cograph’s auto-detect. Class chain: c("htna", "netobject", "cograph_network"). inherits(x, "htna") safe guard. two slots? $nodes$groups keeps cograph happy called directly. $node_groups contract htna enforces. must agree; build_htna() responsible keeping sync. Don’t rely cograph’s auto-detect inside htna. auto-detect (priority list column names: groups, group, clusters, cluster, community, module, layer) generic UX shortcut, contract. htna’s wrappers always pass node_list explicitly call site readable partition source unambiguous.

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: single internal helper returns list cograph::plot_htna parameters. Wrappers merge user overrides via modifyList(). Override discipline: modifyList(defaults, user_args) — user always wins. Don’t conditionally apply (user_args) parameter shape.
code: #' htna visual defaults #' #' Returns the standard htna visual defaults as a named list of cograph #' `plot_htna()` parameters. Used by every htna plot wrapper. #' #' @param net An htna network (built by `build_htna()`). #' @param directed Logical; if NULL, inferred from the network. #' @return Named list of `cograph::plot_htna()` parameters. #' @keywords internal .htna_style_defaults <- function(net, directed = NULL) {   n_groups <- if (!is.null(net$node_groups)) {     length(unique(net$node_groups$group))   } else NA_integer_    if (is.null(directed)) {     directed <- isTRUE(attr(net, "directed")) ||                 isTRUE(net$directed) ||                 !.is_symmetric_matrix(.weights_of(net))   }    defaults <- list(     layout                  = "circular",     # cograph 2.1.1+: circular default     angle_spacing           = 0.35,           # clear inter-group gap     legend                  = TRUE,     legend_position         = "bottom",     legend_horiz            = TRUE,     edge_color              = "#003355",     edge_label_style        = "estimate",     edge_label_leading_zero = FALSE,     edge_label_size         = 0.4,     edge_label_position     = 0.7,     minimum                 = 0.01,     node_size               = 7,     layout_margin           = 0.15,     curvature               = 0.4   )    if (isTRUE(directed)) {     defaults$arrow_size        <- 0.61     defaults$edge_start_length <- 0.2     defaults$edge_start_style  <- "dotted"   }    if (!is.na(n_groups) && n_groups >= 2) {     # Curated palette: starts with the 2-actor anchor (Human=#4FC3F7 /     # AI=#fbb550 from the cograph plot_htna default), then perceptually     # distinct extensions. Override per-call via group_colors.     palette <- c("#4FC3F7", "#fbb550", "#7eb5d6", "#98d4a2",                  "#f4a582", "#92c5de", "#d6c1de", "#b8e186")     shapes  <- c("circle", "square", "diamond", "triangle",                  "pentagon", "hexagon", "star", "cross")     defaults$group_colors <- rep_len(palette, n_groups)     defaults$group_shapes <- rep_len(shapes,  n_groups)   }    defaults }  # Tiny helpers to keep .htna_style_defaults pure ----------------------------- .weights_of <- function(net) {   net$weights %||% as.matrix(net) } .is_symmetric_matrix <- function(m) {   is.matrix(m) && nrow(m) == ncol(m) && isTRUE(all.equal(m, t(m))) } `%||%` <- function(a, b) if (is.null(a)) b else a

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: Every plot wrapper follows shape: entire plot_htna() wrapper. Register export let override current re-export cograph::plot_htna.
code: plot_<thing> <- function(x, ..., .style = .htna_style_defaults) {   # 1. Validate: must be htna   if (!inherits(x, "htna")) {     stop("`x` must be an htna network produced by build_htna().",          call. = FALSE)   }    # 2. Read partition from canonical schema   node_list <- split(x$node_groups$node, x$node_groups$group)    # 3. Merge defaults with user overrides   defaults <- .style(x)   args     <- modifyList(defaults, list(...))    # 4. Always pass node_list explicitly; never rely on auto-detect   do.call(cograph::plot_htna,           c(list(x = x, node_list = node_list), args)) }

what: 5. Layout helper (htna-side port until cograph exports one)
title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: wrappers compose cograph plotters auto-detect groups (splot.net_bootstrap, splot.net_permutation, plot_compare), htna needs compute multi-group layout inject via existing layout = matrix path cograph::splot() already supports. cograph 2.1.1 geometry inside plot_htna() yet export public helper. lands, htna ports minimal version: matrix-injection pattern, used wherever htna wrapper drive cograph plotter doesn’t read $node_groups: Three args together — layout = matrix, rescale = FALSE, layout_scale = 1 — contract lets htna geometry survive cograph’s renderer unchanged. Drop one cograph re-normalizes.
code: #' Compute a circular multi-group layout (htna port) #' #' Mirrors cograph::compute_circular_layout (private). Drop this when cograph #' exports a public `compute_htna_layout()` and switch all callers to that. #' #' @param node_list Named list of character vectors, one per group. #' @param all_labels Character vector of every node label, in network order. #' @param angle_spacing Inter-group gap as fraction of the per-group arc. #' @param scale Radius multiplier. #' @return Named list with x, y numeric vectors aligned to all_labels. #' @keywords internal .htna_circular_layout <- function(node_list, all_labels,                                   angle_spacing = 0.35, scale = 1) {   n_groups <- length(node_list)   group_indices <- lapply(node_list, function(g) match(g, all_labels))    n <- length(all_labels)   x_pos <- rep(0, n); y_pos <- rep(0, n)   radius <- 2 * scale    angle_per_group <- 2 * pi / n_groups   gap_angle       <- angle_per_group * angle_spacing   arc_angle       <- angle_per_group - gap_angle    for (i in seq_len(n_groups)) {     g_idx <- group_indices[[i]]     n_in  <- length(g_idx)     start <- pi/2 - (i - 1) * angle_per_group - gap_angle/2     end   <- start - arc_angle     if (n_in > 1) {       ang <- seq(start, end, length.out = n_in)     } else if (n_in == 1) {       ang <- (start + end) / 2     } else next     x_pos[g_idx] <- radius * cos(ang)     y_pos[g_idx] <- radius * sin(ang)   }   list(x = x_pos, y = y_pos) } node_list  <- split(net$node_groups$node, net$node_groups$group) labels     <- get_nodes(net)$label %||% rownames(weights) xy         <- .htna_circular_layout(node_list, labels, angle_spacing = 0.35) layout_mat <- cbind(x = xy$x, y = xy$y)  # Critical: cograph::splot rescales layouts by default. We pre-rescaled, so # disable both rescaling and layout-scale to preserve our coordinates. args <- modifyList(.htna_style_defaults(net), list(...)) args$layout       <- layout_mat args$rescale      <- FALSE args$layout_scale <- 1  do.call(cograph::<some_plotter>, c(list(x = thing), args))

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: template applies plot_htna_permutation (just call cograph::splot.net_permutation underlying object net_permutation).
code: #' Plot an htna bootstrap result with the htna multi-group layout. #' #' Composes htna styling + the htna multi-group layout with cograph's #' bootstrap renderer. The CI / significance overlay is whatever #' `cograph::splot.net_bootstrap` produces; htna only owns geometry + style. #' #' @param boot Output of `bootstrap_htna()`. #' @param ... Forwarded to `cograph::splot.net_bootstrap`. User args win. #' @export plot_htna_bootstrap <- function(boot, ...) {   if (!inherits(boot, "htna_bootstrap")) {     stop("`boot` must come from bootstrap_htna().", call. = FALSE)   }   net <- boot$model   if (is.null(net$node_groups)) {     stop("boot$model has no $node_groups; was it built by build_htna()?",          call. = FALSE)   }    node_list  <- split(net$node_groups$node, net$node_groups$group)   labels     <- net$nodes$label %||% rownames(net$weights)   xy         <- .htna_circular_layout(node_list, labels, angle_spacing = 0.35)   layout_mat <- cbind(x = xy$x, y = xy$y)    defaults <- .htna_style_defaults(net)   args     <- modifyList(     defaults,     list(layout = layout_mat, rescale = FALSE, layout_scale = 1, ...)   )    do.call(cograph::splot.net_bootstrap, c(list(x = boot), args)) }

what: 7. plot_compare wrapper (TBD until cograph supports it)
title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: cograph’s plot_compare(x, y, ...) currently accept node_list pre-computed layout matrix. either lands upstream, cleanest htna-side implementation avoid wrapping plot_compare directly instead expose difference-network plot via plot_htna: loses cograph’s signed (positive/negative) edge coloring. cograph adds node_list (layout-matrix arg) plot_compare, swap real wrapper preserves diff semantics.
code: plot_htna_diff <- function(x, y, ...) {   if (!inherits(x, "htna") || !inherits(y, "htna")) {     stop("Both `x` and `y` must be htna networks.", call. = FALSE)   }   if (!identical(sort(unique(x$node_groups$group)),                  sort(unique(y$node_groups$group)))) {     stop("x and y must share the same actor partition.", call. = FALSE)   }   diff_mat <- cograph::to_matrix(x) - cograph::to_matrix(y)   rownames(diff_mat) <- colnames(diff_mat) <- x$nodes$label   node_list <- split(x$node_groups$node, x$node_groups$group)   defaults <- .htna_style_defaults(x)   args     <- modifyList(defaults, list(...))   # plot_htna can take a matrix as x and node_list explicitly   do.call(cograph::plot_htna,           c(list(x = diff_mat, node_list = node_list), args)) }

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: package’s value visual. Numerical correctness necessary sufficient. styling layout change ships without HTML visual check. Convention: - Place visual checks tmp/ (gitignore-friendly) inst/visual_checks/. - check renders new output deliberately-old reference side--side multiple group counts (2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8) multiple device aspect ratios (square, wide, tall). - Use rmarkdown::render(). assume agent can see image; ask user review HTML confirm committing.

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: cograph (>= 2.1.1) — required matrix-edge-color fix resolve_edge_colors() (commit 72b7fbc0), MTNA self-loop fixes (commit 7b711e63), legend_position = "bottom" default, angle_spacing = 0.35 circular. cograph exports compute_htna_layout(), drop .htna_circular_layout package import public helper. single planned cograph-side change Phase 2. Nestimate (>= 0.4.3) — required build_htna bootstrap_htna. Verify version r-universe tagging htna release.

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: R list NULL trap: args$x <- NULL deletes element. store NULL, use args["x"] <- list(NULL). Affects modifyList() results default non-NULL user wants NULL. Auto-detect priority list: see Section 2. htna depend , aware calls like cograph::plot_htna(net) (node_list) silently pick whichever group column find first. Pass node_list explicitly unambiguous. splot() dispatch via UseMethod: splot.foo methods found via inherits() cascade splot(). Adding new class cograph recognize means editing cascade. htna need . tna_styling = TRUE psych_styling = TRUE exist cograph legacy reasons. Don’t add htna_styling = TRUE upstream — ’s anti-pattern reasons Section 1.

title: Agent Note for htna — Architecture, Styling, and Plot Wrappers
text: Auto-detect column name design issue moment 0. generic shortcut became de-facto contract accident. htna participate ; always reads $node_groups always passes node_list explicitly. Circular layout htna default. Set .htna_style_defaults(). Bipartite remains available via explicit layout = "bipartite". cograph default also flipped circular 2.1.1. Styling lives htna, cograph. .tna_style_defaults / .psych_style_defaults precedent inside cograph historical drift, pattern follow. plot_htna’s broken “per-axis + dev-aspect stretch” normalization (commit 7b711e63 upstream) rejected. PR #1 sonsoleslp/cograph reverts . htna pin cograph (>= 2.1.1) PR merges.

what: Starting point: a raw event log
title: Data Preparation for HTNA
text: practice, interaction data often arrives single table one row per event. row records actor performed action, action , occurred. simulate Human–AI tutoring interaction actor four distinct codes: can also combine single event log actor column, data often arrives form:
code: library(htna) library(Nestimate)  set.seed(123)  human_codes <- c("Ask", "Confirm", "Revise", "Evaluate") ai_codes    <- c("Suggest", "Explain", "Execute", "Clarify") sessions    <- paste0("S", sprintf("%03d", 1:30))  make_events <- function(codes, sessions, parity) {   rows <- lapply(sessions, function(sid) {     n <- sample(15:25, 1)     data.frame(       session_id       = sid,       code             = sample(codes, n, replace = TRUE),       order_in_session = seq_len(n) * 2L - parity,       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   })   do.call(rbind, rows) }  human_data <- make_events(human_codes, sessions, 1L) ai_data    <- make_events(ai_codes, sessions, 0L) events <- rbind(   data.frame(actor = "Human", human_data, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   data.frame(actor = "AI", ai_data, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) )  events$actor = factor(events$actor, levels = c("Human","AI")) events <- events[order(events$session_id, events$order_in_session), ] head(events[, c("actor", "session_id", "code", "order_in_session")]) #>     actor session_id    code order_in_session #> 1   Human       S001 Confirm                1 #> 616    AI       S001 Explain                2 #> 2   Human       S001  Revise                3 #> 617    AI       S001 Execute                4 #> 3   Human       S001 Confirm                5 #> 618    AI       S001 Execute                6

text: data already long format session order columns, can pass directly build_htna() using actor_col argument:

text: data actor stored separately (prepared independently), pass named list:

what: Option 3: Prepare each actor with Nestimate::prepare()
title: Data Preparation for HTNA
text: raw data needs cleaning – e.g. session detection timestamps, tie-breaking, format conversion – can use Nestimate::prepare() actor’s data independently, feed prepared long-format data build_htna(). prepare() returns list several components. long_data element contains cleaned long-format data ready network estimation: Pass prepared long data named list:
code: prep_human <- prepare(   human_data,   actor   = "session_id",   action  = "code",   session = "session_id",   order   = "order_in_session" )  prep_ai <- prepare(   ai_data,   actor   = "session_id",   action  = "code",   session = "session_id",   order   = "order_in_session" ) head(prep_human$long_data[, c("session_id", "code", "order_in_session")]) #>   session_id    code order_in_session #> 1       S001 Confirm                1 #> 2       S001  Revise                3 #> 3       S001 Confirm                5 #> 4       S001 Confirm                7 #> 5       S001 Confirm                9 #> 6       S001  Revise               11 net3 <- build_htna(   list(Human = prep_human$long_data, AI = prep_ai$long_data) ) net3 #> Transition Network (relative probabilities) [directed] #>   Weights: [0.006, 0.308]  |  mean: 0.140 #>  #>   Weight matrix: #>              Ask Clarify Confirm Evaluate Execute Explain Revise Suggest #>   Ask      0.013   0.248   0.013    0.040   0.215   0.275  0.013   0.181 #>   Clarify  0.273   0.045   0.240    0.214   0.013   0.032  0.175   0.006 #>   Confirm  0.024   0.183   0.012    0.006   0.268   0.207  0.018   0.280 #>   Evaluate 0.021   0.210   0.035    0.007   0.308   0.189  0.007   0.224 #>   Execute  0.191   0.013   0.270    0.230   0.013   0.013  0.257   0.013 #>   Explain  0.194   0.035   0.215    0.229   0.021   0.028  0.271   0.007 #>   Revise   0.014   0.295   0.007    0.027   0.199   0.226  0.021   0.212 #>   Suggest  0.250   0.014   0.293    0.186   0.014   0.021  0.193   0.029  #>  #>   Initial probabilities: #>   Ask           0.300  ████████████████████████████████████████ #>   Revise        0.267  ████████████████████████████████████ #>   Evaluate      0.233  ███████████████████████████████ #>   Confirm       0.200  ███████████████████████████ #>   Clarify       0.000   #>   Execute       0.000   #>   Explain       0.000   #>   Suggest       0.000 plot_htna(net3, layout = "auto")

title: Data Preparation for HTNA
text: three approaches produce network underlying data equivalent. Choose whichever fits data pipeline.

title: Data Preparation for HTNA
text: different actors share code labels (e.g. Human AI code called “Ask”), build_htna() raise error default. Set disambiguate = TRUE automatically prefix codes actor name:
code: net <- build_htna(data, actor_col = "actor", disambiguate = TRUE) # Codes become "Human:Ask", "AI:Ask", etc.

title: Getting Started with htna
text: Start loading package example data shipped Nestimate: build_htna() accepts named list long-format data frames, one per actor group. combines sequences, estimates transition probabilities, stores actor partition resulting network object: actor partition available two forms: net$nodes$groups – actor label per node (auto-detected plot_htna()). net$node_groups – canonical (node, group) data frame used cograph::get_groups() cograph::cluster_summary().

title: Getting Started with htna
text: plot_htna() auto-detects actor groups renders distinct colours. defaults circular layout 0.05 edge threshold:

title: Getting Started with htna
text: Meta-paths type-level patterns abstract away concrete states ask often typed template appears data. example, Human->AI->Human captures every length-3 path starts Human code, transitions AI code, returns Human code. can search specific schema (wildcards): Gapped meta-paths allow skipping positions: Filter lift find -represented meta-paths:
code: # Enumerate all meta-paths of length 2 to 4 mp <- extract_meta_paths(net) mp #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 28 | Lengths: 2, 3, 4 | Gaps: 0 #>            schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>         Human->AI      2   0  5970   428   0.998     0.316 1.28 #>         AI->Human      2   0  5693   424   0.988     0.301 1.22 #>      Human->Human      2   0  4674   422   0.984     0.247 0.79 #>            AI->AI      2   0  2581   403   0.939     0.136 0.70 #>  Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.194 1.41 #>  Human->Human->AI      3   0  3172   422   0.984     0.172 1.25 #>  AI->Human->Human      3   0  2828   403   0.939     0.153 1.11 #>     AI->Human->AI      3   0  2744   383   0.893     0.148 1.36 #>     Human->AI->AI      3   0  2189   403   0.939     0.118 1.09 #>     AI->AI->Human      3   0  2100   397   0.925     0.114 1.04 #> ... (18 more) # Any length-3 path that returns to Human extract_meta_paths(net, schema = "Human->*->Human") #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 2 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 0 #>               schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>     Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.709 1.41 #>  Human->Human->Human      3   0  1472   396   0.923     0.291 0.46 extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, type = "gapped", gap = 1:2) #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 16 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 1, 2 #>               schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>     Human->AI->Human      3   1  3265   408   0.951     0.185 1.35 #>     Human->Human->AI      3   1  2722   413   0.963     0.154 1.12 #>        AI->Human->AI      3   1  2515   379   0.883     0.143 1.31 #>     AI->Human->Human      3   1  2242   391   0.911     0.127 0.92 #>  Human->Human->Human      3   1  2146   372   0.867     0.122 0.70 #>        Human->AI->AI      3   1  1748   384   0.895     0.099 0.91 #>        AI->AI->Human      3   1  1618   360   0.839     0.092 0.84 #>           AI->AI->AI      3   1  1382   304   0.709     0.078 0.91 #>  Human->Human->Human      3   2  2948   399   0.930     0.176 1.01 #>     Human->Human->AI      3   2  2341   390   0.909     0.139 1.01 #> ... (6 more) extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, min_lift = 1.2) #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 3 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 0 #>            schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>  Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.194 1.41 #>  Human->Human->AI      3   0  3172   422   0.984     0.172 1.25 #>     AI->Human->AI      3   0  2744   383   0.893     0.148 1.36

title: Multi-Group HTNA (3+ Actor Groups)
text: simulate collaborative coding session three actors – Student, AI assistant, System (e.g. IDE automated tooling) – four distinct codes:
code: library(htna)  set.seed(42)  student_codes <- c("Ask", "Confirm", "Revise", "Evaluate") ai_codes      <- c("Suggest", "Explain", "Execute", "Clarify") system_codes  <- c("Log", "Notify", "Compile", "Sync") sessions      <- paste0("S", sprintf("%03d", 1:30))  make_events <- function(codes, sessions, offset) {   rows <- lapply(sessions, function(sid) {     n <- sample(15:25, 1)     data.frame(       session_id       = sid,       code             = sample(codes, n, replace = TRUE),       order_in_session = seq_len(n) * 3L - offset,       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   })   do.call(rbind, rows) }  student_data <- make_events(student_codes, sessions, 2L) ai_data      <- make_events(ai_codes, sessions, 1L) system_data  <- make_events(system_codes, sessions, 0L)

title: Multi-Group HTNA (3+ Actor Groups)
text: Pass three groups named list: actor partition now contains three levels:

title: Multi-Group HTNA (3+ Actor Groups)
text: three groups, plot_htna() automatically switches polygon (triangle) layout. three colours default HTNA palette applied order:  can switch layouts well:

title: Multi-Group HTNA (3+ Actor Groups)
text: Meta-paths now span three actor types. example, can search paths System mediates Student AI: enumerate length-3 meta-paths:
code: extract_meta_paths(net3, schema = "Student->System->AI") #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 30 sequences #> (no rows met the filters) extract_meta_paths(net3, length = 3) #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 30 sequences #> Rows: 17 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 0 #>                    schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>       Student->AI->System      3   0   510    30   1.000     0.295 7.98 #>       AI->System->Student      3   0   497    30   1.000     0.288 7.78 #>       System->Student->AI      3   0   487    30   1.000     0.282 7.62 #>            AI->System->AI      3   0    41    10   0.333     0.024 0.62 #>        System->AI->System      3   0    37    10   0.333     0.021 0.56 #>   System->Student->System      3   0    32    10   0.333     0.019 0.50 #>  Student->System->Student      3   0    27     9   0.300     0.016 0.44 #>      Student->AI->Student      3   0    24     7   0.233     0.014 0.39 #>           AI->Student->AI      3   0    24     7   0.233     0.014 0.38 #>    System->System->System      3   0    16     7   0.233     0.009 0.24 #> ... title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Calling plot_htna() extra arguments uses htna defaults: circular layout, 0.05 edge threshold, built-colour palette.
code: plot_htna(m) #> Registered S3 methods overwritten by 'cograph': #>   method             from      #>   plot.net_stability Nestimate #>   print.mcml         Nestimate

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: layout parameter controls two groups arranged space.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Places group vertical column, cross-group edges drawn . classic bipartite layout.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Distributes nodes along perimeter circle, alternating groups. Good emphasising overall connectivity pattern.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: orientation parameter rotates rearranges bipartite layout. applies layout = "bipartite" ("auto" 2 groups).

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Groups placed two vertical columns, side side.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Groups stacked two horizontal rows, one .

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Similar vertical, two columns face nodes aligned rank, making easier trace individual edges.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Arranges group along semicircle, groups together form full circle.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: group_spacing parameter controls horizontal distance two columns. Larger values push groups apart, can help reduce edge overlap dense networks.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: node_spacing parameter controls vertical gap nodes within group. Increase node labels overlap; decrease compact plot.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: columns parameter splits group multiple sub-columns. useful group many nodes single column tall. scalar applies groups; length-2 vector sets group independently.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: using multiple columns, column_spacing adjusts horizontal gap sub-columns within group.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: default (use_list_order = TRUE), nodes appear order found data. Setting FALSE reorders nodes edge weight, placing connected nodes centre group.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: curvature parameter controls much cross-group edges bend. 0 produces straight lines, higher values increase arc. Useful reducing overlapping edges.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: intra_curvature parameter adds extra curvature edges nodes group, arcing away opposite group. visually separates within-group -group transitions.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: vector colours, one per group. recommended approach scales number groups.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: parameters still work two-group networks generalise three groups.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: default, edge colours derived (darkened) group colours source node.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Supply vector colours override automatic derivation.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Set edge_colors = FALSE use single default colour edges.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: group_shapes parameter assigns different node shape group, adding second visual channel beyond colour. Common shapes include "circle", "square", "diamond", "triangle".

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Jitter shifts nodes horizontally based connectivity, breaking rigid column alignment. Nodes stronger cross-group edges move closer group, giving visual indication coupling strength.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: numeric value controls maximum displacement.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: default, first group jittered. Set jitter_side = "" apply jitter groups.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: legend_position parameter accepts standard R positions: "bottomright" (default), "bottomleft", "topleft", "topright".

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Draw extension lines outward node, can help connect labels nodes dense plots. TRUE uses default length; numeric value sets length explicitly.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: layout_margin parameter adds padding around plot area. Increase labels clipped edges.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: scale parameter adjusts spacing proportionally, useful exporting higher resolutions. value 2 doubles distances.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: label_abbrev parameter truncates node labels fixed number characters (numeric) lets function choose automatically ("auto"). Helpful code names long.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Pass data frame label column (matching) labels column (display) rename nodes plot without modifying network.
code: nodes_df <- data.frame(   label  = c("left", "right"),   labels = paste0("N", 1:10) ) plot_htna(m, nodes = nodes_df)

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: angle_spacing parameter controls empty space corners circular polygon layouts. Higher values create larger gaps vertices, visually separating groups.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: additional arguments forwarded cograph::splot() via .... gives access full set rendering options.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: threshold parameter (passed splot) hides edges weights given value.

title: plot_htna: 2-Group Parameter Gallery
text: Parameters can freely combined fine-tune visualisation:
code: plot_htna(m,           group_colors = c("#E91E63", "#009688"),           group_shapes = c("circle", "diamond"),           group_spacing = 6,           jitter = TRUE,           jitter_side = "both",           curvature = 0.6,           threshold = 0.08,           legend_position = "topright")

title: Handoff — htna agent-note implementation, Phase 1
text: note specs node_size = 7, edge_color, edge_label_size, edge_label_position .htna_style_defaults(). cograph 2.1.2’s actual chain plot_htna → tplot → splot, tplot translates dotted formal names (vsize, edge.color, edge.label.cex, edge.label.position) splot’s underscored args concatenating .... Passing underscored names htna therefore collides tplot already set — formal argument "X" matched multiple actual arguments. fix R/style_defaults.R use tplot’s formal names ’re absorbed cleanly: cograph 2.2 lands plot_htna calls splot directly (skipping tplot), revert underscored names note specs.

title: Handoff — htna agent-note implementation, Phase 1
text: R/reexports.R exists working tree (untracked, dated 2026-04-27) competing plot_htna definition. bypasses cograph::plot_htna entirely calls cograph::splot() directly custom layout matrix, custom palette (htna_palette: #FFD966 #B4A7D6 #93C47D #E06666 #6FA8DC #F6B26B), darkened donut borders (.darken()), custom graphics::legend() renderer. R loads files alphabetically, reexports.R overwrites Section 4 wrapper R/plot_htna.R. file HEAD — HEAD’s reexports.R original 3-line cograph::plot_htna re-export. Someone (likely Sonsoles, given timestamps) wrote alternative implementation agent-note draft now. Decision needed can ship: Adopt agent note written. Delete reexports.R; keep R/plot_htna.R. Loses: custom palette, donut darkening, custom legend, left-starting circular layout. Wins: rest architecture (.htna_style_defaults, layout port, bootstrap/diff wrappers) compose cograph::plot_htna entry point — note spent Section 1 arguing right choice. Adopt reexports.R written. Delete R/plot_htna.R. note’s architecture (Sections 3–7) becomes inert — bootstrap diff wrappers particular call cograph::plot_htna need rewriting splot-direct path. Merge. Keep Section 4 wrapper, fold visual choices reexports.R .htna_style_defaults(): palette → defaults$group_colors donut darkening → add defaults$donut_color left-starting circular → flip layout port start pi instead pi/2 custom legend → drop (cograph’s legend = TRUE + legend_position = "bottom" note’s choice) option ’d recommend one strong feelings bypassing plot_htna. preserves note’s anti-coupling argument (Section 1) keeping visual identity Sonsoles already chose.

title: Handoff — htna agent-note implementation, Phase 1
text: Per Section 10: Phase 2 — plot_htna_permutation. template plot_htna_bootstrap cograph::splot.net_permutation. note defers pending cograph::compute_htna_layout(), ’s writable today .htna_circular_layout. Phase 3 — real plot_compare wrapper preserving signed-difference rendering. Gated cograph adding node_list / layout-matrix support plot_compare. Phase 4 — find_patterns(). Referenced roxygen extract_meta_paths print.htna_paths unimplemented. print method already supports level = "state" ("Patterns (state-level)"). Either ship function strip dangling references. Phase 5 — tests + README. package neither.

title: Handoff — htna agent-note implementation, Phase 1
text: Worth recording future-adding agent note’s Section 11 quirks list: importFrom(cograph, plot_htna) shadows local plot_htna htna namespace. wrapper R/plot_htna.R calls cograph::plot_htna() namespaced, import unnecessary harmful. Already removed. tplot’s name translation table (Section “Deviations” ) single biggest gotcha writing wrappers. default uses splot’s underscored name collide tplot also sets . safe--construction approach : pass tplot’s preferred name tplot formal concept, use splot’s name things tplot doesn’t touch (arrow_size, edge_start_length, edge_start_style, legend_horiz, edge_label_style, edge_label_leading_zero). splot.netobject sets tna_styling = TRUE default network’s method c("relative", "frequency", "attention", "co_occurrence", "wtna", "wtna_cooccurrence"). activates whole block internal defaults inside splot (lines 196–235 cograph::splot). defaults intentionally re-set htna look deterministic regardless method, future change splot’s .tna_style_defaults silently change htna visuals.

title: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis
text: htna R package Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis (HTNA) models processes interactions mix two actor groups (e.g.  Human AI) single network. HTNA builds traditions Transition Network Analysis (TNA) Co-occurrence Network Analysis (CNA) maintains rigor either method. package provides focused API top Nestimate estimation engine cograph rendering engine: build network combined sequence preserving actor partition, downstream bootstrap, permutation, reliability, centrality, plotting functions treat actor’s codes distinct node group.

title: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis
text: can install development version htna GitHub:

title: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis
text: Load example data shipped Nestimate:

title: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis
text: build_htna() takes named list long-format data frames, one per actor group, combines sequences, estimates transition probabilities, stores actor partition resulting network:

title: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis
text: plot_htna() auto-detects actor groups renders distinct colours:
code: plot_htna(net, threshold = 0.05, layout = "circular") #> Using 'groups' column for node groups

title: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis
text: Meta-paths type-level patterns abstract away concrete states. example, Human->AI->Human captures every length-3 path starts Human code, transitions AI code, returns Human code: Search specific schema wildcards:
code: extract_meta_paths(net) #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 28 | Lengths: 2, 3, 4 | Gaps: 0 #>            schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>         Human->AI      2   0  5970   428   0.998     0.316 1.28 #>         AI->Human      2   0  5693   424   0.988     0.301 1.22 #>      Human->Human      2   0  4674   422   0.984     0.247 0.79 #>            AI->AI      2   0  2581   403   0.939     0.136 0.70 #>  Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.194 1.41 #>  Human->Human->AI      3   0  3172   422   0.984     0.172 1.25 #>  AI->Human->Human      3   0  2828   403   0.939     0.153 1.11 #>     AI->Human->AI      3   0  2744   383   0.893     0.148 1.36 #>     Human->AI->AI      3   0  2189   403   0.939     0.118 1.09 #>     AI->AI->Human      3   0  2100   397   0.925     0.114 1.04 #> ... (18 more) extract_meta_paths(net, schema = "Human->*->Human") #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 2 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 0 #>               schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>     Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.709 1.41 #>  Human->Human->Human      3   0  1472   396   0.923     0.291 0.46

title: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis
text: tna – Transition Network Analysis homogeneous sequences. Nestimate – Network estimation, bootstrap, permutation, reliability, centrality. cograph – Network visualisation rendering. codyna – Sequence patterns, outcomes, indices.

title: Bootstrap an HTNA Network — bootstrap_htna
text: Thin wrapper around Nestimate::bootstrap_network() runs standard state-level edge bootstrap htna network tags result identifiable htna bootstrap. bootstrap inference Nestimate's: per-edge mean, sd, p-value, significance, confidence / credibility intervals across iter resamples. State-level granularity correct level edge-stability analysis - aggregating actor pairs smear real edge--edge differences.

title: Bootstrap an HTNA Network — bootstrap_htna
text: x [htna] network built build_htna(). ... Arguments forwarded Nestimate::bootstrap_network() (e.g. iter, ci_level, consistency_range, seed).

title: Bootstrap an HTNA Network — bootstrap_htna
text: object class c("htna_bootstrap", "net_bootstrap"). slots produced Nestimate::bootstrap_network() preserved verbatim ($summary, $mean, $sd, $p_values, $significant, $ci_lower/$ci_upper, $cr_lower/$cr_upper, $model, $original, etc.).

title: Bootstrap an HTNA Network — bootstrap_htna
text: wrapper adds identity downstream class continuity: returned object class "htna_bootstrap" ahead "net_bootstrap", inherits(boot, "htna_bootstrap") works. actor partition ($nodes$groups, $node_groups) restored boot$model input network cograph's auto-detect still recognizes heterogeneous schema downstream. boot$model re-tagged "htna" front class chain chain c("htna", "netobject", "cograph_network") - identical build_htna() returns.

title: Bootstrap an HTNA Network — bootstrap_htna
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net  <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)) boot <- bootstrap_htna(net, iter = 200) inherits(boot, "htna_bootstrap")            # TRUE inherits(boot$model, "htna")                # TRUE head(boot$summary)                          # state-level edge stability } # }

what: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
text: Builds transition network combined sequence two actor groups (e.g. Human AI) preserves actor partition result downstream plotting analysis can treat actor's codes distinct node group.

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
code: build_htna(   data,   actor_col = NULL,   action = "code",   session = "session_id",   order = "order_in_session",   method = "relative",   disambiguate = FALSE,   ... )

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
text: data Either: named list long-format data frames, one per actor (e.g. list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)). frames must share column schema. single long-format data frame actor column. case actor_col must supplied. actor_col Character. Name actor column data single data frame. Ignored data named list. action Character. Name action/code column. Default "code". session Character. Name session column. Default "session_id". order Character. Name within-session order column. Default "order_in_session". method Character. Transition method passed build_network: "relative" (default), "frequency", "attention". disambiguate Logical. FALSE (default), function errors code label appears one actor group. TRUE, codes prefixed actor name ("Human:Ask", "AI:Ask") become distinct nodes. ... Additional arguments forwarded build_network.

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
text: netobject actor partition stored cograph's canonical schema, cograph::plot_htna(net) auto-detects groups arguments: $nodes$groups - actor label per node (column name cograph::plot_htna auto-detects). $node_groups - data frame columns node group (canonical cograph_network schema, also readable cograph::get_groups, cluster_summary, print method cograph_network). slots exactly returned build_network.

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)) net$node_groups            # canonical (node, group) schema cograph::plot_htna(net)    # auto-detects $nodes$groups, no other args } # }

what: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: Discovers type-level patterns (meta-paths) actor-typed sequences produced heterogeneous transition network. meta-path fixed sequence node types - example Human->AI->Human - abstracts away concrete states asks often typed template appears data. Two regimes supported: enumerate every meta-path given lengths (default), search one specific schema (optional wildcards).

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
code: extract_meta_paths(   x,   length = 2:4,   schema = NULL,   type = c("contiguous", "gapped"),   gap = 1L,   min_count = 1L,   min_support = 0,   min_lift = 0,   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   contain = NULL )

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: x [htna_network] network built build_htna(). Must $nodes$groups, $node_groups, $data populated. length [integer(): 2:4] Meta-path lengths enumerate. Ignored schema supplied. schema [character(1)] Optional. meta-path template search , written actor names separated "->" (e.g. "Human->AI->Human"). Wildcards supported: "Human->*->Human" matches every length-3 meta-path starts ends Human, type . type [character(1): "contiguous"]"contiguous" (default) considers consecutive positions; "gapped" considers positions spaced gap + 1 apart. gap [integer(): 1L] Gap size(s) used type = "gapped". Multiple values produce one row per (length, gap) combination, distinguished gap column. min_count [integer(1): 1L] Minimum total occurrences retain. min_support [numeric(1): 0] Minimum proportion sequences containing meta-path least . min_lift [numeric(1): 0] Minimum lift (observed / expected marginal independence). 0 disables filter. start [character()] Keep meta-paths whose first type set. end [character()] Keep meta-paths whose last type set. contain [character()] Keep meta-paths whose type sequence contains elements set (order).

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: object class c("htna_meta_paths", "htna_paths", "data.frame") columns: schema, length, gap, count, n_sequences, support, frequency, lift. Attributes: n_sequences, alphabet, level = "meta".

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: Operates actor partition stored network build_htna().

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long))  # All meta-paths of length 2..4 extract_meta_paths(net)  # Wildcard family search: any length-3 path that returns to Human extract_meta_paths(net, schema = "Human->*->Human")  # Gapped meta-paths extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, type = "gapped", gap = 1:2)  # Lift threshold (over-represented relative to type marginals) extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, min_lift = 1.2) } # }

what: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
title: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
text: Build, analyze, visualize transition networks sequences mix two actor groups (e.g. Human AI) within interaction.

title: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
text: package thin layer top two foundations: Nestimate - estimation, bootstrap, permutation, reliability, centrality, Markov machinery. cograph - rendering (splot, plot_htna, transitions, communities, chord, heatmap). htna adds heterogeneous-actor wrapper build_htna re-exports commonly used downstream functions users need attach Nestimate cograph explicitly.

title: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
text: Maintainer: Mohammed Saqr saqr@saqr.[copyright holder]

what: Plot a Heterogeneous Transition Network — plot_htna
title: Plot a Heterogeneous Transition Network — plot_htna
text: htna's wrapper around cograph::plot_htna(). Reads actor partition canonical $node_groups schema written build_htna(), applies htna visual defaults, forwards everything cograph::plot_htna() node_list passed explicitly (reliance cograph's column-name auto-detection). Wrapper around cograph::plot_htna() defaults suited HTNA networks built build_htna(). layout = "circular", custom layout computed first actor group appears left.

title: Plot a Heterogeneous Transition Network — plot_htna
code: plot_htna(x, layout = "circular", group_colors = htna_palette, ...)  plot_htna(x, layout = "circular", group_colors = htna_palette, ...)

title: Plot a Heterogeneous Transition Network — plot_htna
text: x network object produced build_htna(). layout Character. Layout algorithm. Default "circular". group_colors Character vector colours, one per actor group. Defaults built-htna_palette. ... Additional arguments passed cograph::plot_htna().

title: Plot a Heterogeneous Transition Network — plot_htna
text: value returned cograph::plot_htna() (invisibly). Called side effect (plot). Returns x invisibly.

title: Plot a Heterogeneous Transition Network — plot_htna
text: User arguments ... always win defaults.

title: Plot a Heterogeneous Transition Network — plot_htna
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)) plot_htna(net) plot_htna(net, threshold = 0.1, legend = FALSE) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)) plot_htna(net) plot_htna(net, layout = "auto", threshold = 0.1) } # }

what: Plot an htna Bootstrap Result — plot_htna_bootstrap
title: Plot an htna Bootstrap Result — plot_htna_bootstrap
text: Composes htna's visual defaults htna multi-group circular layout cograph's bootstrap renderer (cograph::splot.net_bootstrap()). CI / significance overlay whatever cograph produces; htna owns geometry visual identity (bootstrap plot looks like network plot_htna()).

title: Plot an htna Bootstrap Result — plot_htna_bootstrap
Plot an htna Bootstrap Result — plot_htna_bootstrap
text: boot object returned bootstrap_htna(). ... Forwarded cograph::splot.net_bootstrap(). User args win htna defaults.

title: Plot an htna Bootstrap Result — plot_htna_bootstrap
text: value returned cograph::splot.net_bootstrap() (invisibly).

title: Plot an htna Bootstrap Result — plot_htna_bootstrap
text: layout computed htna side injected matrix together rescale = FALSE layout_scale = 1. three arguments together contract lets htna geometry survive cograph's renderer unchanged - drop one cograph re-normalizes.

title: Plot an htna Bootstrap Result — plot_htna_bootstrap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net  <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)) boot <- bootstrap_htna(net, iter = 200) plot_htna_bootstrap(boot) plot_htna_bootstrap(boot, display = "significant") } # }

what: Plot the Difference Between Two htna Networks — plot_htna_diff
title: Plot the Difference Between Two htna Networks — plot_htna_diff
text: Renders elementwise edge-weight difference x - y heterogeneous transition network using plot_htna(). networks must share actor partition (group labels $node_groups).

title: Plot the Difference Between Two htna Networks — plot_htna_diff
Plot the Difference Between Two htna Networks — plot_htna_diff
text: x, y htna networks produced build_htna() matching actor partitions. ... Forwarded cograph::plot_htna(). User args win.

title: Plot the Difference Between Two htna Networks — plot_htna_diff
text: value returned cograph::plot_htna() (invisibly).

title: Plot the Difference Between Two htna Networks — plot_htna_diff
text: htna-side workaround cograph::plot_compare(), (yet) accept node_list pre-computed multi-group layout. result, signed (positive / negative) edge coloring plot_compare provides upstream lost - edges colored plot_htna() call. cograph adds node_list (layout-matrix argument) plot_compare, swap implementation real plot_compare wrapper preserves diff semantics.

title: Plot the Difference Between Two htna Networks — plot_htna_diff
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net1 <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long,        AI = ai_long)) net2 <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long[1:50,], AI = ai_long)) plot_htna_diff(net1, net2) } # }

title: Print Method for htna Path/Pattern Objects — print.htna_paths
text: input object, invisibly.
